pattern: Extending each starting index one element at a time keeps a running least common multiple, counting a hit whenever it equals the target and pruning once it overshoots.
hints: Fix a left endpoint and grow the subarray rightward, updating a running LCM as you go.
The LCM only ever increases as you add elements, so once it exceeds the target you can stop extending that start.
For each start, accumulate lcm with each new element; whenever it equals k add one to the count, and break as soon as it surpasses k.

edge_inputs.description: several scattered subarrays reach the target lcm
target larger than any reachable lcm
an element bigger than k blocks any subarray crossing it
ones extend a valid lcm without changing it
k is one, only all-one subarrays count
values divide k but never combine to exactly k

stress.description: moderate array over a small range with a target that has many divisor combinations
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 12) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.choice([6, 12, 4, 8, 1, 60])
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: highly composite targets create many candidate subarrays
